metadata.name: bullpeg
metadata.description: Bullpeg: Solana port of Unipeg's trade-as-mint NFT primitive

instructions.name: initialize
docs: One-time setup: creates the BullBank singleton PDA and locks the
$TOKEN mint address. The mint is immutable thereafter — switching
mints requires a fresh deployment.

accounts.name: program
docs: This program's account — used to locate its ProgramData.

accounts.name: program_data
docs: The program's upgrade-authority record. Only the wallet that holds
the upgrade authority of *this* deployed program may initialize.

instructions.name: initialize_collection
docs: One-time setup: creates the Metaplex Certified Collection (MCC)
parent NFT. Must be called by `bank.authority` exactly once. After
this, every wrap_bull verifies its NFT into this collection so
Magic Eden / Tensor / Phantom recognise the bulls as a real
collection (no DYOR warnings, searchable by collection, floor
pricing surfaces).

accounts.name: authority
docs: Deployer wallet — pays rent for collection mint, ATA, metadata,
master edition. Must equal bank.authority.

accounts.name: collection_mint
docs: Fresh keypair for the collection NFT mint (caller-generated).
Initialized here with decimals=0; mint+freeze authority is the
program-controlled `collection_authority` PDA so we can sign
verify CPIs for every future wrap_bull.

accounts.name: collection_authority
docs: Program PDA that signs as Metaplex update_authority + mint authority
for the collection NFT. Same PDA signs verify_sized_collection_item
for every wrap_bull, so the program (not a wallet) is the authority.

accounts.name: authority_collection_ata
docs: Deployer's ATA for the collection NFT (receives the 1-supply mint).
Holding the collection NFT in the deployer's wallet is the standard
Tensor/ME pattern and lets the deployer claim the collection on
Creator Hub / Tensor Creator Portal.

accounts.name: collection_metadata
docs: Metaplex metadata account for the collection NFT.

accounts.name: collection_master_edition
docs: Metaplex master edition account for the collection NFT.

instructions.name: unwrap_bull
docs: Unwrap a bull NFT, releasing the locked 1M $TOKEN to the caller.
Caller must hold 1 of the bull's NFT in their ATA. Verified on-chain.
After this instruction:
- 1M $TOKEN flows from vault → caller
- NFT mint, ATA, metadata, master edition, and BullAsset are closed
- tier_index goes back onto the free_tiers stack for reuse
- Reused tiers re-roll their visual (new NFT mint = new seed)

accounts.name: payer
docs: The unwrapping wallet. Must hold the bull NFT in their ATA.
Receives the 1M $TOKEN drained from the vault and the rent from
the closed vault + bull_asset accounts.

accounts.name: payer_token_account
docs: Caller's $TOKEN account — receives the unwrapped 1M tokens.
Boxed to keep stack frame under Solana's 4096-byte limit.

accounts.name: token_mint
docs: $TOKEN mint (must match bank.token_mint).

accounts.name: nft_mint
docs: The bull's NFT mint.
Must match the BullAsset record. After burn_nft this account is closed.

accounts.name: nft_mint_authority
docs: Vault authority PDA (also the NFT mint authority before master edition
took over). Used to sign the vault drain + close.

accounts.name: vault
docs: Vault holding the locked 1M $TOKEN. ATA owned by `nft_mint_authority`.
Drained then closed in this instruction; rent goes to payer.

accounts.name: payer_nft_account
docs: Caller's NFT ATA — must hold 1 of nft_mint (proves ownership).
Closed by burn_nft.

accounts.name: bull_asset
docs: BullAsset record. Closed at the end of this ix; rent → payer.

accounts.name: metadata
docs: Metaplex metadata account (closed by burn_nft).

accounts.name: master_edition
docs: Metaplex master edition (closed by burn_nft).

accounts.name: collection_mint
docs: Collection NFT mint (must equal `bank.collection_mint`).
Required for the burn_nft CPI on verified collection members.

accounts.name: collection_metadata
docs: Collection NFT's Metaplex metadata account (mut — burn decrements size).

instructions.name: wrap_bull
docs: Wrap 1M $TOKEN into a fresh bull NFT.
Caller must:
1. Generate a fresh keypair for the NFT mint client-side
2. Pre-compute the next tier_index from BullBank state
3. Sign as both `payer` and `nft_mint` keypair
The 1M tokens are transferred from the caller's $TOKEN account into
a vault PDA derived from the new NFT mint. The vault's authority is
`PDA(["vault", nft_mint])`, so whoever holds the NFT controls the
vault via this program. The Metaplex NFT is fully tradeable on
Magic Eden / Tensor / any standard marketplace.

accounts.name: payer
docs: The wrapping wallet. Signs to authorize:
- 1M $TOKEN transfer out of their token account
- rent payment for new NFT mint, vault, ATAs, and BullAsset PDA

accounts.name: payer_token_account
docs: Caller's $TOKEN account (source of the 1M tokens being locked).
Boxed to keep the WrapBull stack frame under Solana's 4096-byte limit
(this struct has 15 accounts; un-boxed they'd blow the stack).

accounts.name: token_mint
docs: $TOKEN mint (must match bank.token_mint — locked at initialize).

accounts.name: nft_mint
docs: NFT mint for this bull. PDA derived from the bank's pre-increment
`total_wrapped` value, so each wrap gets a unique mint address
without requiring an external Keypair signer client-side. This is
the single-signer pattern Phantom's docs recommend (Domain and
transaction warnings, bullet 1) and lets Lighthouse simulate the
tx cleanly without flagging it as a suspicious multi-signer pattern.
Re-roll-on-tier-reuse is preserved: total_wrapped monotonically
increases, so re-wrapped tier #42 (at total_wrapped=42) and
re-wrapped tier #42 (later at total_wrapped=N) have different mint
addresses and therefore different generative visuals.

accounts.name: nft_mint_authority
docs: PDA derived from the NFT mint. This single PDA serves as:
- the NFT mint's mint+freeze authority (until master edition takes over)
- the vault token account's authority (so vault tokens follow the NFT)

accounts.name: vault
docs: Vault: ATA owned by `nft_mint_authority` PDA, holding the 1M $TOKEN.
Address is deterministic — anyone can compute it from (nft_mint, $TOKEN).
When the NFT is sold on a marketplace, the new owner controls the
`nft_mint_authority` PDA (via this program), so the vault tokens
follow the NFT atomically.

accounts.name: payer_nft_account
docs: Caller's ATA for the new NFT — receives 1 of the freshly minted NFT.

accounts.name: bull_asset
docs: BullAsset record — created here, closed on unwrap.
Seed: ["bull", tier_index_le_bytes]. Init failure protects against
race conditions (two simultaneous wraps cannot claim the same tier).

accounts.name: metadata
docs: Metaplex metadata account for the NFT.
(it requires the standard PDA derivation).

accounts.name: master_edition
docs: Metaplex master edition account (limits supply to 1).

accounts.name: collection_mint
docs: Collection NFT mint (must equal `bank.collection_mint`).
Constraint blocks any wrap before the collection is initialized
(CollectionNotInitialized — bank.collection_mint == default).
verify CPI revalidates everything else.

accounts.name: collection_metadata
docs: Collection NFT's Metaplex metadata account.

accounts.name: collection_master_edition
docs: Collection NFT's Metaplex master edition account.

accounts.name: collection_authority
docs: Program PDA that signs verify_sized_collection_item as the
collection's update_authority. Same PDA established by
initialize_collection.

errors.name: WrongMint
errors.msg: Token account is not for the configured $TOKEN mint

errors.name: InsufficientBalance
errors.msg: Caller's balance is below the 1,000,000 token wrap threshold

errors.name: NotNftHolder
errors.msg: Caller does not hold the bull NFT being unwrapped

errors.name: VaultMismatch
errors.msg: Vault account does not match the expected PDA for this NFT mint

errors.name: MaxBullsReached
errors.msg: Maximum bulls already wrapped (1,000 cap reached)

errors.name: TierMismatch
errors.msg: Bull asset PDA does not match expected tier index

errors.name: TierOutOfBounds
errors.msg: Tier index out of bounds (must be 1..=1000)

errors.name: NftMintMismatch
errors.msg: Provided NFT mint does not match the BullAsset record

errors.name: VaultBalanceMismatch
errors.msg: Vault balance is not exactly 1,000,000 tokens

errors.name: CollectionNotInitialized
errors.msg: Metaplex Certified Collection not initialized — call initialize_collection first

errors.name: WrongCollection
errors.msg: Provided collection mint does not match BullBank.collection_mint

errors.name: NotProgramAuthority
errors.msg: Caller is not the program authority recorded in BullBank

errors.name: UnauthorizedInitializer
errors.msg: Only the program's upgrade authority may call initialize

types.name: BullAsset
docs: Per-bull PDA created on wrap, closed on unwrap (rent reclaimed).
Seed: ["bull", tier_index_le_bytes]
Each BullAsset is paired with:
- an NFT mint at the address `nft_mint` (Metaplex Token Metadata NFT)
- a vault token account at PDA(["vault", nft_mint]) holding 1M $TOKEN
The vault's authority is the same PDA derived from the NFT mint, so when
the NFT trades on a marketplace the vault's tokens follow the new owner
(whoever holds the NFT can sign as the vault PDA via this program).
The deterministic SVG renderer seeds from `nft_mint` so the visual is
locked at wrap time and stays consistent through transfers. When a tier
is reused after unwrap, a fresh nft_mint is generated → new visual.

fields.name: nft_mint
docs: NFT mint address (Metaplex NFT representing this bull).
The vault and visual are both derived from this address.

fields.name: tier_index
docs: Tier index 1..=MAX_BULLS (the "CryptoBulls #N" public identifier).

fields.name: wrapped_at
docs: Wrap timestamp (unix seconds).

fields.name: bump
docs: PDA bump.

types.name: BullBank
docs: Singleton PDA holding global protocol state.
Seed: ["bank"]
Tracks tier lifecycle (next_tier counter + free_tiers stack), wrap/unwrap
counters, and the locked $TOKEN mint that this bullpeg deployment wraps.
Note: no `#[derive(Default)]` because `[u8; 64]` does not implement
Default in Rust's std. Anchor's `init` constraint zero-initializes the
account, and the handler explicitly populates every field.

fields.name: token_mint
docs: $TOKEN mint address (set at initialize, immutable thereafter).
Pump.fun-launched tokens have null mint authority, so this is always
safe to lock — the mint can never be inflated/changed.

fields.name: total_wrapped
docs: Total bulls wrapped across protocol lifetime (lifetime counter).

fields.name: total_unwrapped
docs: Total bulls unwrapped across protocol lifetime.

fields.name: in_circulation
docs: Currently in-circulation bulls (= total_wrapped - total_unwrapped).

fields.name: next_tier
docs: Lowest tier that has never been wrapped. Starts at 1; increments after
each wrap that drains a "fresh" tier (i.e., free_tiers is empty).
When `next_tier > MAX_BULLS` and `free_tiers` is empty, no more bulls
can be wrapped (cap reached).

fields.name: free_tiers
docs: Stack of tiers that were once wrapped, then unwrapped, and are now
awaiting reuse. On wrap: pop here first; if empty, take from
next_tier counter. On unwrap: push tier here. Reused tiers re-roll
their visual because the new wrap creates a fresh NFT mint.

fields.name: authority
docs: Admin authority (program upgrade only — token side is pump.fun-immutable).

fields.name: bump
docs: PDA bump.

fields.name: collection_mint
docs: Metaplex Certified Collection (MCC) NFT mint. Set by
`initialize_collection` after `initialize`. Pubkey::default() until
then. Each `wrap_bull` references this and verifies the freshly
minted bull NFT into this collection (so Magic Eden / Tensor /
Phantom recognise the collection).
Layout note: this slot was carved out of the original 64-byte
`reserved` block, so the on-chain account size is unchanged. Existing
banks deserialize this field as Pubkey::default() (all zeros) until
initialize_collection writes it.

fields.name: reserved
docs: Reserved for future fields. Do not remove without state migration.
